name: Charred Zucchini Verde Royale with Pistachio Tarator & Burnt Lemon Gel
source: inspired by Osteria Francescana (Modena, Italy) and Noma (Copenhagen, Denmark)
description: A Michelin-level zucchini side dish featuring vacuum-compressed zucchini charred over binchotan charcoal, layered with pistachio tarator, burnt lemon gel, frozen basil snow, and crispy zucchini blossoms. A modern vegetable composition balancing smoke, acidity, creaminess, and temperature contrast.
keywords: zucchini, fine dining, Michelin, pistachio tarator, burnt lemon, basil snow, vegetable tasting menu, modern gastronomy

winePairings.name: Etna Bianco
winePairings.notes: Mineral-driven Sicilian white with volcanic salinity that complements the charred zucchini and pistachio.

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne Blanc de Blancs
winePairings.notes: High acidity and fine mousse elevate the basil, smoke, and citrus components.

steps.instruction: Slice the baby zucchini lengthwise and place into vacuum bags with olive oil, thyme, lemon zest, and a small pinch of salt.
Vacuum seal at full compression and refrigerate for 1 hour to densify texture.
Char the lemons directly over open flame until nearly blackened.
Juice the charred lemons into a saucepan with water and sugar. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid, whisk in agar agar, bring briefly to a boil, then chill until set. Blend into a smooth burnt lemon gel and transfer to a squeeze bottle.
Toast pistachios lightly and crush with roasted garlic and salt using a volcanic mortar.
Blend pistachios with tahini, yogurt, lemon juice, olive oil, and enough ice water to achieve a silky consistency.
Roast zucchini trim and shallot until caramelized. Deglaze with white wine, add vegetable stock, and reduce by 75%.
Strain the glaze and whisk in olive oil and sherry vinegar. Infuse with olive wood smoke using a smoking gun.
Blanch basil for 10 seconds, shock in ice water, blend smooth, and freeze using an anti-griddle or Pacojet beaker.
Mix rice flour, sparkling water, and salt into a light batter. Dip zucchini blossoms and fry at 375°F until crisp.
Heat binchotan charcoal until extremely hot. Grill zucchini aggressively on cut sides only until charred outside but bright green inside.
Brush zucchini continuously with smoked zucchini glaze while grilling.
Blend parsley, basil, chives, and grapeseed oil into a vibrant herb oil and strain finely.
Plate with an asymmetrical swipe of pistachio tarator, layered zucchini, dots of burnt lemon gel, crispy blossom shards, basil snow, herb oil, lemon zest, and micro herbs.
Crush pistachios, garlic, and salt using a mortar.
Blend with yogurt, tahini, lemon juice, and olive oil until silky smooth.
Simmer charred lemon juice with water and sugar.
Whisk in agar agar, boil briefly, chill until set, then blend smooth.
